Name: Alakazam
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another as long as you don’t Knock Out that Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all Energy attached to Charizard into [R] Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can’t be used if Charizard is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

Name: Dark Blastoise
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Dark Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 40 damage in this way.
Dark Blastoise does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Dark Blastoise during your opponent’s next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flavor-Text: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

Name: Dark Dragonite
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: When you play Dark Dragonite from your hand, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Sometimes called “The God of Destruction.” Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

Name: Dark Persian
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This attack can’t be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

Name: Dark Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, flip another coin. If the second coins is heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If the second coin is tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Stores up electricity in its body, then suddenly releases it to surprise and shock everyone.

Name: Dark Slowbro
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: When you play Dark Slowbro from your hand, choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards from your discard pile and put them into your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

Name: Dark Vaporeon
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon capable of controlling water, so it is capable of creating giant whirlpools.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Flareon or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

Name: Gengar
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can’t be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Under a full moon, this Pokémon like to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon, and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It accumulates negative ions from the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

Name: Machamp
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Whenever your opponent’s attack damages Machamp (even if Machamp is Knocked Out), this power does 10 damage to the attacking Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power can’t be used if Machamp is already affected by a Special Condition when your opponent attacks.
Flavor-Text: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

Name: Muk
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Ignore all Pokémon Powers other than Toxic Gases. This power stops working while Muk is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.
Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Ninetales or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Venusaur
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 [G] Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can’t be used if Venusaur is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.
Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal of half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Butterfree has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

Name: Electrode
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon isn’t Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

Name: Golem
Effects: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player’s Bench. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Golem does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.

Name: Hypno
Effects: Look at up to 3 cards from the top of either player’s deck and rearrange them as you like.
If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

Name: Kabutops
Effects: Remove a number of damage counters from Kabutops equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Kabutops has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
Flavor-Text: Its sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)
Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player’s Bench. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and attach it to another of your opponent’s Pokémon of your choice.

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)
Flavor-Text: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

Name: Moltres
Effects: You may discard any number of [R] Energy cards attached to Moltres when you use this attack. If you do, discard that many cards from the top of your opponent’s deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player’s turn (even if it was already Poisoned).
Flavor-Text: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey’s bones.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.
Flavor-Text: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

Name: Pidgeotto
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is Knocked Out.)
Flavor-Text: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Arcanine or this attack does nothing.
Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Charmeleon or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

Name: Dark Dragonair
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for an Evolution card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Dark Dragonair is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Flavor-Text: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

Name: Dark Wartortle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
If an attack does damage to Dark Wartortle during your opponent’s next turn (even if Dark Wartortle is Knocked Out), Dark Wartortle does an equal amount of damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a negative attitude, it hides in its shell whenever it’s in a bad mood—which is often.

Name: Dewgong
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

Name: Dodrio
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: As long as Dodrio is Benched, pay [C] less to retreat your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dodrio.
Flavor-Text: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Fearow.
Flavor-Text: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.
Flavor-Text: Often seen swimming elegantly by lake shores. It is often mistaken for the Japanese monster, Kappa.

Name: Graveler
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Graveler (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flavor-Text: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

Name: Haunter
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Whenever an attack does anything to Haunter, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Haunter. This power stops working while Haunter is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When the bulb on its back grows large, the Pokémon seems to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

Name: Kabuto
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Whenever an attack (even your own) does damage to Kabuto (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does only half the damage to Kabuto (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power stops working while Kabuto is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.

Name: Kadabra
Effects: Discard 1 [P] Energy card attached to Kadabra or this attack does nothing. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.
Flavor-Text: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent’s next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.
Machoke does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Metapod
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent’s next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The female’s horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

Name: Omanyte
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Your opponent plays with his or her hand face up. This power stops working while Omanyte is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Omanyte but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.

Name: Omastar
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Omastar but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A prehistoric Pokémon that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible for it to catch prey.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If tails, Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: Always furious and tenacious to boot. It will not abandon chasing its quarry until its quarry is caught.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rapidash.
Flavor-Text: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hope of racing it.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon’s remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).
Flavor-Text: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Seadra but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Seadra.
Flavor-Text: Capable of swimming backward by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.

Name: Snorlax
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Snorlax can’t become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Burned. This power stops working while Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).
Flavor-Text: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, the are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.

Name: Abra
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.
Flavor-Text: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

Name: Caterpie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard 1 [R] Energy card attached to Charmander or this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

Name: Dratini
Flavor-Text: Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.

Name: Drowzee
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep; if tails, your Active Pokémon is now Asleep. The power can’t be used if Drowzee is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack Eevee during your opponent’s next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.
Flavor-Text: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Put up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. Gastly does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A mysterious Pokémon. Some say it is a lifeform from another dimension, while others believe it is formed from smog.

Name: Geodude
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
All damage done by attacks to Grimer during your opponent’s next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Appears in filthy areas. Thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: It’s said that not even pro wrestlers can take down a Machop.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player’s Bench. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Shuffle your opponent’s deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.
Flavor-Text: Normally friendly, it quickly becomes angry if it doesn’t get what it wants.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Nidoran Female or Nidoran Male and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can’t use this attack if your Bench is full.)
Flavor-Text: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

Name: Onix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flavor-Text: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything flat in moments.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Draw a card.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Psyduck but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: It is often seen holding its head in its paws, as if it were suffering from a headache.

Name: Rattata
Flavor-Text: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)
Flavor-Text: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Burrow deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove a damage counter from Slowpoke. This attack can’t be used if Slowpoke has no damage counters on it.
Discard 1 [P] Energy card attached to Slowpoke in order to use this attack. Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Incredibly slow and dopey. It takes 5 seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Spearow
Effects: If Spearow was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Spearow to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speed to stay airborne.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent’s next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)
Flavor-Text: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

Name: Tentacool
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may return Tentacool to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to Tentacool.) This power can’t be used the turn you put Tentacool into play or if Tentacool is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

Name: Full Heal Energy
Description: If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer affected by a Special Condition. Full Heal Energy provides [C] energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Potion Energy
Description: If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any. Potion Energy provides [C] energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Pokémon Breeder
Description: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can play this card only when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.

Name: Pokémon Trader
Description: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Scoop Up
Description: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)

Name: The Boss’s Way
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution card with Dark in its name. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Challenge!
Description: Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can’t do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.

Name: Mysterious Fossil
Description: Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks, can’t retreat, and can’t be affected by Special Conditions. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play. You can put Pokémon that evolve from Mysterious Fossil on this card.

Name: Potion
Description: Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
